title: rubus corylifolius subsp. lagerbergii
intro: Rubus corylifolius subsp. lagerbergii, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a type of plant species in the Rubus genus, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus corylifolius subsp. lagerbergii include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and used in capsules or tinctures, making it a versatile medicinal resource.
